channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Kansas secretary of state launches 2026 GOP gubernatorial bid for seat held by 2-term Democrat
item.description: Secretary of State Scott Schwab is running for governor of Kansas in 2026, launching his campaign exclusively with Fox News Digital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some Texas business leaders are apprehensive about Trump’s pledged deportations
item.description: “We wouldn’t survive” without undocumented workers, one South Texas produce business owner said. By one estimate, 8% of Texas’ workforce lacks legal status.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Alejandra Martinez, Alejandro Serrano, Berenice Garcia, Carlos Nogueras Ramos And Joshua Fechter, KPRC 2

item.title: An 'extraordinary man': Former President Carter lies in state at Capitol ahead of state funeral
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter died Sunday, Dec. 29, at 100 years old. His death came just over a year after the death of his wife of 77 years, Rosalynn Carter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden admin appeals plea agreements with Guantánamo detainees, including 9/11 mastermind
item.description: The Biden administration is asking for a federal appeals court to temporarily block a plea deal agreement with three detainees in Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump refuses to rule out use of military force to take control of Greenland and the Panama Canal
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday said he would not rule out the use of military force to seize control of the Panama Canal and Greenland, as he declared U.S. control of both to be vital to American national security.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Future of Florida's shores: Biden's offshore oil drilling ban faces political backlash
item.description: President Joe Biden issued a ban on offshore oil drilling in federal waters, including the Gulf of Mexico. But President-elect Donald Trump plans to reverse the move when he takes office in less than two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks release of special counsel report on Trump cases as court fight simmers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday temporarily blocked the public release of special counsel Jack Smith's report on investigations into Donald Trump as an appeals court weighs a challenge to the disclosure of a much-anticipated document just days before the president-elect reclaims office.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Sen. Warren presses Hegseth to answer questions about his past actions and statements
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Sen. Elizabeth Warren is pressing Pete Hegseth, President-elect Donald Trump's pick for defense secretary, to answer additional questions about his past actions and statements before next Tuesday's confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Carolina justices block certification of election outcome in race for one of its own seats
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — North Carolina's highest court blocked on Tuesday the certification of a November election result for one of its own seats so it can review legal arguments by a trailing candidate who contends over 60,000 ballots that were cast shouldn't be counted.
item.dc:creator: GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: RFK Jr. to meet with slew of Dems including Elizabeth Warren, Bernie Sanders
item.description: RFK Jr. begins meeting with Senate Democrats this week as his coalition for getting confirmed to HHS secretary remains uncertain.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Outgoing WH official calls for US to bolster cybersecurity workforce by hiring non-degree holders
item.description: The White House’s outgoing cyber czar Harry Coker called more funding, deregulation and opening up cyber jobs to those without college degrees.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden administration asks court to block plea deal for alleged mastermind of 9/11 attacks
item.description: The Biden administration has asked a federal appeals court to block a plea agreement for accused 9/11 mastermind Khalid Sheikh Mohammed that would spare him the risk of the death penalty.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know about Trump and his keen interest in Greenland
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has said multiple times that the U.S. should buy Greenland, an autonomous territory of Denmark. The sparsely populated island is geopolitically important and mineral-rich.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: The president who couldn't quit: Jimmy Carter's foreign policy legacy goes beyond the White House
item.description: Jimmy Carter has a foreign policy legacy that wasn't just defined by his four years in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thune tees up Tuberville's bill to block transgender athletes from women’s sports
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) on Tuesday took the necessary procedural steps to place Sen. Tommy Tuberville’s (R-Ala.) bill blocking transgender athletes from women’s sports on the Senate calendar. The step sets up a vote on Tuberville’s bill in the weeks ahead.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: 'All hell will break out' if Hamas hostages not released by inauguration
item.description: "It will not be good for Hamas, and it will not be good, frankly, for anyone," Trump said during a press conference at his Mar-a-Lago residence. "All hell will break out. I don't have to say any more, but that's what it is.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: US says Sudanese rebel force has committed genocide and imposes sanctions on the group's leaders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Tuesday that a Sudanese paramilitary group and its proxies are committing genocide in a civil war with the country's military that has killed tens of thousands of people, leveling sanctions on the group’s leader and affiliated companies.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate approves committee assignments, tees up confirmation hearings next week
item.description: The Senate on Tuesday approved its organizing resolution for the 119th Congress, officially setting the composition of committees and greenlighting nomination hearings to take place in the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Board of Supervisors splits votes on new chair following Vargas' exit
item.description: The San Diego County Board of Supervisors punted a vote on who will succeed Nora Vargas as chair after competing motions to elevate either Terra Lawson-Remer or Jim Desmond to the role failed in votes down partisan lines.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Harris will travel to Asia, Mideast and Europe during her final week in office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris plans to close out her term with an around-the-world trip making stops in Singapore, Bahrain and Germany, her office said. The trip, which is scheduled to last from Jan. 13 to Jan. 17, will be a final opportunity for Harris to address U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-President Jimmy Carter's body arrives in Washington
item.description: The next phase of former President Jimmy Carter's state funeral has begun as his body is transported to Washington.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: Trump won't commit to not using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: “Look, the Panama Canal is vital to our country, it’s being operated by China, China. And we gave the Panama Canal to Panama, we didn’t give it to China,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson: ‘Intention’ is to increase debt limit in massive Trump agenda bill
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said congressional Republicans plan to increase the nation’s borrowing limit as a part of a massive party-line bill they are crafting that will encapsulate President-elect Trump’s legislative agenda.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin Republicans pushing to make voter ID a constitutional requirement
item.description: Other states have taken similar steps in recent years to put voter ID requirements in the constitution. Voters approved it in Mississippi in 2011 and North Carolina voters in 2018, while Minnesota voters rejected it in 2012.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Appeals court rejects Trump’s latest attempt to get Friday’s hush money sentencing called off
item.description: In a one-sentence ruling following an emergency hearing, Judge Ellen Gesmer denied Trump’s request for an immediate order that would spare him from being sentenced while he appeals Judge Juan M. Merchan’s decision last week to uphold the historic verdict.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: California governor proposes $322B budget with no deficit after two years of shortfalls
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has proposed a $322 billion budget without a deficit, a welcome change after two years of significant budget shortfalls in the nation's most populous state.
item.dc:creator: Tran Nguyen, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US transfers 11 Guantanamo detainees to Oman after more than two decades without charge
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon said Monday it had transferred 11 Yemeni men to Oman this week after holding them for more than two decades without charge at the U.S. naval base in Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota lawmakers prepare to take office
item.description: The Minnesota House will enter the 2025 legislative session with a new power structure and also a new home base down the block as its typical digs undergo construction.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump Jr. plans Greenland visit after father stirs controversy
item.description: Donald Trump Jr. is planning to head to Greenland for a day-long trip just weeks after his father, President-elect Trump, said U.S. ownership of the Danish territory "is an absolute necessity.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, Fox 5

item.title: Biden tells New Orleans mourners they are not alone as he honors victims of attack
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — President Joe Biden told mourners in New Orleans on Monday that they are not alone as he paid tribute to victims of the deadly New Year’s attack and channeled the pain felt by their loved ones. Biden made the remarks at St. Louis Cathedral in the city's historic French Quarter.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill O'Reilly was in a 'Cabinet meeting' during Mar-a-lago visit
item.description: While Trump's approach is high-risk, O'Reilly argues it reflects his commitment to his agenda and disregard for political norms.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: British prime minister condemns ‘lies and misinformation’ after Musk attacks
item.description: Elon Musk has criticized Keir Starmer, called for new elections in the U.K. and voiced support for far-right figures in the country.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Judge holds Giuliani in contempt in Georgia election workers case
item.description: A federal judge held Rudy Giuliani in civil contempt on Monday for failing to comply with court orders in two former Georgia election workers’ efforts to collect their $146 million defamation judgment. At the end of a two-day hearing that saw Giuliani take the stand, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Child tax credit expansion proposed: What it means for New Yorkers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A proposal to expand New York's child tax credit would give families more money per child, Gov. Kathy Hochul announced on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: A Pulitzer winner quits Washington Post after a cartoon on Bezos is killed
item.description: Washington Post cartoonist Ann Telnaes resigned after an editor rejected her sketch satirizing tech chiefs, including the Post's owner and Amazon founder Jeff Bezos.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: New laws in NJ impact minimum wage, data privacy, and reading content
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) -- With every new year come new laws -- each impacting someone somewhere in the Garden State. New Jersey's minimum wage is increasing to $15.49 an hour for most workers.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11
